name: @forwardimpact/pathway
description: Explore roles, generate agent profiles, and surface career expectations so progression and team configuration are visible, not guessed.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: A promotion decision stalls because two managers disagree on what 'senior' means — neither can point to a written definition.
jobs.bigHire: give specialized roles coherent expectations from a shared foundation so evaluations feel fair rather than arbitrary.
jobs.littleHire: define what a new role requires without starting from a blank page.
jobs.competesWith: tribal knowledge; borrowed frameworks; per-manager intuition; tolerating the ambiguity

forces.pull: A shared definition that makes quality visible and discussable.
forces.habit: Each manager carries a private mental model of what 'good' means.
forces.anxiety: Getting definitions wrong feels worse than having none.

jobs.firedWhen: definitions drift from practice; or a reorg removes the mandate to maintain them.

jobs.trigger: A post-mortem surfaces the same skill gap that caused the last incident — nobody had a way to see it before staffing the team.
jobs.bigHire: see what each role on the team actually requires so staffing isn't guesswork.
jobs.littleHire: check whether a candidate fills the team's actual gap, not just the job description.
jobs.competesWith: résumé screening; gut feel; copying a team that seems to work; accepting gaps as inevitable

forces.push: Capability gaps appear as incidents, never in advance.
forces.pull: Confidence that a staffing change strengthens the team as a system.
forces.habit: Hiring on résumés and gut rather than team composition analysis.

jobs.firedWhen: the skill taxonomy drifts from actual roles; or a hiring freeze removes the pressure to staff analytically.

jobs.trigger: Starting a new role and realizing that 'meets expectations' on the review form has no definition anyone can point to.
jobs.bigHire: see exactly what's expected at my level so I stop guessing during reviews.
jobs.littleHire: understand the autonomy and scope my level implies so I can self-assess honestly.
jobs.competesWith: asking peers; inferring from manager comments; browsing LinkedIn postings for similar titles; living with the ambiguity

forces.push: Ambiguous expectations create anxiety about reviews and readiness.
forces.pull: Clarity about what's expected, removing the guesswork from self-assessment.
forces.habit: Inferring expectations from peer behavior rather than a standard.
forces.anxiety: Looking up the standard might confirm gaps easier left ignored.

jobs.firedWhen: defined expectations diverge from actual promotions; or a reorganization makes the framework feel irrelevant.

jobs.trigger: An agent delivered work the team rejected because it followed generic best practices instead of the organization's actual standards.
jobs.bigHire: configure agents to meet the same expectations the organization holds for human contributors.
jobs.littleHire: give agents organizational context without writing bespoke prompts for every task.
jobs.competesWith: writing custom system prompts; copying another team's agent config; hoping generic instructions produce aligned output; not configuring agents at all

forces.push: Agent output gets rejected or reworked because generic configuration doesn't match what the organization expects.
forces.pull: Agents that reflect organizational standards without bespoke prompt engineering for each task.
forces.habit: Treating agent configuration as a one-time setup rather than something that evolves with standards.
forces.anxiety: Over-constraining agents might make them less useful for novel or unexpected tasks.

jobs.firedWhen: configuration overhead exceeds the quality gain; or a policy change bans custom agent configuration.

jobs.trigger: Reviewing agent output line by line because there's no objective way to tell whether it meets the organization's quality bar without reading everything.
jobs.bigHire: know what to expect from agent output so I can review by exception, not by default.
jobs.littleHire: check the role's expected output before reviewing so I know what to look for.
jobs.competesWith: reviewing every line agents produce; accepting slower delivery as the cost of using agents; trusting output uncritically and catching issues later; not using agents for substantive work

forces.push: Full review of every agent deliverable negates the productivity gain of using agents.
forces.pull: Confident delegation — knowing when agent output meets the standard without reading every line.
forces.habit: Reviewing everything because that's how quality has always been caught.
forces.anxiety: Delegating quality judgment to a standard might miss context-specific issues a review would catch.

jobs.firedWhen: standards-based verification catches fewer real issues than full review; or the team stops using agents for substantive work.
